rationale: Gate-safety survives the 6-bead resequencing. Verified on disk: adr_citations unchanged (0037/0018/0023/0025, ALL Accepted) and jointly cover every impacted domain (workflow←0037/0023/0025; core←0018; execution←0037/0025; context-system←0018) → adr-coverage passes, no adr-cite-irrelevant. ADR-0022 (Proposed) is a spec touchpoint only, not a plan cite, and divergence reads plan adr_citations → harmless. (2) Bead 3's new gitutil+executor surfaces are execution-owned (internal/executor/**, internal/gitutil/** both in execution OWNERSHIP); net-new internal/layout/** is claimed by workflow IN Bead 3 → no unowned. (3) Bead 4's mindspec_executor.go is execution-owned. (4) Bead 5 depends_on[2,3,4]; Bead 2 lands ALL additive multi-prefix matchers in Phase 1 — the linchpin isDocFile flat-prefix (so moved docs/OWNERSHIP manifests classify as docs not source), the ref-anchored LoadOwnershipAtRef/domainManifestRelPath pair, project-docs/** non-source, and the additive root review/** + <spec-dir>/reviews/** both-non-source — so the irreversible move stays green. ADR-0039 lands Proposed-but-uncited in Bead 6 → no adr-divergence-proposed. (5) Permanently multi-prefix matchers + atomic move+self-glob update in Bead 5 keep domains resolvable throughout. No new gate hole.

findings.issue: Re-verified post-split: adr_citations (0037/0018/0023/0025) are byte-unchanged and all Accepted on disk; domain coverage map (workflow/core/execution/context-system) is fully satisfied and every cite intersects an impacted domain. The 6-bead split moved NO matcher out of Bead 2 and did not alter the citation set. divergence reads plan-frontmatter adr_citations, so the Proposed ADR-0022 spec-touchpoint and the not-yet-created ADR-0039 are both gate-irrelevant.

findings.area: ownership coverage of new Bead-3/Bead-4 seams
findings.issue: NEW Bead 3 surfaces internal/gitutil + internal/executor (GitMv/ResetHard/CleanForce/ref-discovery on the Executor interface) — both packages are execution-owned (internal/executor/**, internal/gitutil/** in execution OWNERSHIP), covered by cited Accepted 0037+0025 → adr-coverage passes, no adr-divergence-unowned. Net-new internal/layout/** is claimed by workflow within Bead 3 (step 6), so it is owned at the same complete (AC21). Bead 4's merge guard in internal/executor/mindspec_executor.go is likewise execution-owned. The resequencing introduces no unowned source.

Bead 5 depends_on [2,3,4]; Bead 2 (Phase 1) lands every additive multi-prefix matcher BEFORE the move: confirmed current isProcessArtifact carries HasPrefix(path,"review/") which Bead 2 keeps as a permanent matcher and supplements with a /reviews/ segment matcher; isDocFile is made flat-prefix-aware so the moved .mindspec/{specs,adr,domains,core} docs and the flattened OWNERSHIP manifests classify as docs (the linchpin that keeps doc-sync + adr-divergence-unowned green at the move); project-docs/** classifies non-source. So the one-way cut, dogfood eviction, and root review/** migration all stay green at Bead 5 complete.

findings.area: self-glob resequencing into the move/cleanup beads
findings.issue: Moving the domain OWNERSHIP self-glob update into Bead 5 keeps domains resolvable throughout because Bead 2's matchers (LoadOwnershipAtRef/domainManifestRelPath + listDomainDirs/TreeDirsAtRef) are PERMANENTLY multi-prefix (accept canonical AND flat). The manifest move + self-glob rewrite land atomically in Bead 5's commit, so no complete-point ever sees a manifest moved-to-flat-but-still-claiming-canonical. Bead 3's earlier internal/layout claim on the still-canonical workflow OWNERSHIP.yaml is gate-safe under the unmodified canonical matchers (Bead 3 touches no flat path), consistent with Bead 3 being a depends_on:[] root.

Carried from round 1: ADR-0039 is created via `mindspec adr create` in Bead 6, which defaults Status: Proposed, and the plan leaves its terminal status open. Gate-safe (uncited → no adr-divergence-proposed), but a Proposed ADR-0039 describing an already-shipped live layout is a governance inconsistency (`mindspec adr list --status proposed` will surface it). Recommend the plan state whether ADR-0039 lands Accepted in Bead 6 or is flipped at final-review (spec-105 pattern). Not a blocker; deferrable to impl.

findings.area: ADR-0037 amendment timing vs Bead-4 panel code
findings.issue: Carried from round 1, re-framed for the split: the ADR-0037 reviews-location amendment now lands in Bead 6, two beads after Bead 4 ships the spec-scoped panel scan and Bead 5 migrates the reviews. This is gate-safe — doc-sync (ADR-0031) gates code↔domain-overview/interfaces sync, NOT ADR amendment timing — and is actually MORE correct (the amendment describes the SHIPPED flat tree). Confirm Bead 4's panel/complete code change needs no same-bead workflow domain-overview/interfaces doc update. Observation only.

Carried from round 1: context-system's complete-gate coverage rests solely on ADR-0018 (Lean Bootstrap). Mechanically valid — ADR-0018 declares context-system and Bead-2's budgeter.go is context-system-owned → coveredAccepted. The split did not change this. The Bead-2 budgeter accessor refactor is loosely related to ADR-0018's subject, but domain-level (not change-level) coverage passes cleanly. Informational.
